input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
You're most likely missing the **`BindingContext`** assignment. The `{Binding}` markup extension needs a source object to pull values from.

**Two common fixes:**

**1. Set it in code-behind:**
```csharp
public partial class MyPage : ContentPage
{
    public MyPage()
    {
        InitializeComponent();
        BindingContext = new MyViewModel();
    }
}
```

**2. Or set it in XAML:**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:YourApp.ViewModels"
             x:DataType="vm:MyViewModel">
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:MyViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```

Also ensure your ViewModel implements `INotifyPropertyChanged` (or uses `[ObservableProperty]` from the MVVM Community Toolkit) so the UI updates when property values change.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. The labels are blank even though the ViewModel properties have values. This is a classic problem — they're missing the `BindingContext` assignment.
